title: Conditional Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, and Batch · Type-System Trace: Rename Nonatomic
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing conditional TypeScript mutation protocol; use readonly mutation intents, exact source versions, concurrency preconditions, per-item results, and exhaustive partial-failure handling. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rename nonatomic increment in conditional TypeScript mutation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Rename Nonatomic · Theory Rename Nonatomic
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1635 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to model a version-aware non-atomic copy, validate, and delete rename workflow; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Rename Nonatomic model · Theory Rename Nonatomic, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: S3 rename is one atomic operation like filesystem rename. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
